ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "id_employee" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the employee */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the employee */,
  "hire_date" TEXT /* Date the employee was hired */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the employee */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the employee */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the employee belongs to */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the employee */,
  "active_status" INTEGER /* Indicates if the employee is currently active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id_employee")
)

CREATE TABLE "offices" (
  "office_code" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each office */,
  "office_name" TEXT /* Name of the office location */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical address or description of the office's location */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Maximum number of employees the office can accommodate */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the manager responsible for the office */,
  "open_hours" TEXT /* Operating hours of the office */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("office_code"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_offices_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES employees ("id_employee")
)

CREATE TABLE "attendance_records" (
  "id_employee" INTEGER /* ID of the employee whose attendance is being recorded */,
  "post_code" INTEGER /* Post code associated with the employee's office assignment */,
  "office_code" INTEGER /* Office code where the employee is assigned */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the employee started their work session */,
  "finish_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the employee finished their work session or NULL if ongoing */,
  "shift_type" TEXT /* Type of shift (e.g., morning, evening) */,
  "workstation_id" INTEGER /* ID of the workstation assigned to the employee */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the attendance record (approved, pending, rejected) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id_employee", "start_date"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_attendance_records_post_code FOREIGN KEY ("post_code") REFERENCES post_codes ("post_code"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_attendance_records_office_code FOREIGN KEY ("office_code") REFERENCES offices ("office_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "post_codes" (
  "post_code" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each post code */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description or details about the post code */,
  "region" TEXT /* Broader geographical region */,
  "city" TEXT /* City associated with the post code */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("post_code")
)
